name: Illinois SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Provides monthly food benefits to low-income individuals and families via the Illinois Link card. Apply online through ABE or at a local Family Community Resource Center.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households
hours: DHS Help Line: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Illinois Application for Benefits Eligibility (ABE)
description: Online portal to apply for SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, and other public assistance benefits in Illinois. Single application for multiple programs.
eligibilitySummary: Illinois residents seeking public assistance benefits
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Illinois Medicaid
description: Free or low-cost health coverage for low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities in Illinois.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults up to 138% FPL, children up to 318% FPL, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults: 138% FPL; Children (All Kids): up to 318% FPL; varies by category
hours: DHS Help Line: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM

name: All Kids Health Insurance
description: Illinois health insurance program for children. Covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, vision, dental, and mental health services for uninsured children.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children in Illinois under age 19, regardless of immigration status
incomeLimitNotes: Children up to 318% FPL; premiums and co-pays may apply above 200% FPL

name: Get Covered Illinois (Health Insurance Marketplace)
description: Illinois' official state-based health insurance marketplace for enrolling in ACA health and dental coverage, with financial assistance for qualifying individuals.
eligibilitySummary: Illinois residents not eligible for Medicaid and without employer coverage
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households 100-400% FPL

name: Illinois Department of Employment Security (IDES) - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. File claims and certify for weekly benefits online.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during base period; benefits based on prior earnings

name: IDES Jobs & Workforce Services
description: Job search assistance, career counseling, resume help, and employment resources through Illinois' network of employment offices.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Illinois job seekers

name: Illinois workNet
description: Connects job seekers, employers, and workforce professionals to career, employment, and training resources. Find local American Job Centers, explore careers, and access training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Illinois residents seeking employment and training resources
hours: Online portal available 24/7; in-person centers vary by location

name: Illinois Housing Development Authority (IHDA)
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable housing programs, rental assistance, homebuyer assistance, and foreclosure prevention resources.
address: 111 E. Wacker Drive, Suite 1000, Chicago, IL 60601
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income Illinois residents
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: Illinois Housing Help (IHDA Rental Assistance Portal)
description: Online portal for Illinois rental assistance programs including the Court-Based Rental Assistance Program (CBRAP) administered by IHDA.
eligibilitySummary: Tenants facing eviction or housing instability who meet income requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 80% AMI

name: Chicago Housing Authority (CHA)
description: Provides public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) to low-income residents of Chicago. Manages public housing developments and project-based voucher programs.
address: 60 E. Van Buren Street, Chicago, IL 60605
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Chicago residents; selected from waitlist
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for initial eligibility

name: Legal Aid Chicago
description: Provides free civil legal services to people living in poverty in Cook County. Covers housing, family law, public benefits, consumer rights, and immigration.
address: 120 S. LaSalle Street, Suite 900, Chicago, IL 60603
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Cook County
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125-200% FPL

name: Prairie State Legal Services
description: Free civil legal assistance to low-income people across 36 counties in Northern and Central Illinois. Handles housing, family, consumer, and public benefits cases.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents in 36 Northern and Central Illinois counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125-200% FPL

name: Land of Lincoln Legal Aid
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income residents in Central and Southern Illinois. Covers housing, family law, public benefits, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Central and Southern Illinois
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125-200% FPL
hours: Legal Advice and Referral Center: Mon-Thu 9 AM - 4 PM, Fri 9 AM - 1:30 PM

name: Illinois Legal Aid Online (ILAO)
description: Statewide website providing free legal information, self-help tools, court forms, and referrals to legal aid organizations for Illinois residents.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Illinois residents seeking legal information and referrals

name: Illinois LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps eligible low-income households pay heating and electric bills. Provides one-time payments to energy providers on behalf of qualifying households.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% FPL based on household size
hours: LIHEAP Helpline available during business hours

name: Help Illinois Families (Energy Assistance Portal)
description: Online application portal for LIHEAP energy assistance and other utility bill help programs in Illinois. Apply for heating, cooling, and weatherization assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Illinois households struggling with utility bills
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% FPL

name: Illinois 211
description: Free, confidential helpline connecting people to local health and human service resources. Available 24/7 by phone, text, email, or online chat.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Illinois residents
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: 211 Metro Chicago
description: Connects residents in the Chicago metro area to local health and human services including housing, food, mental health, substance use, and more.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Cook County and surrounding metro area
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Greater Chicago Food Depository
description: Chicago's food bank, distributing food to a network of 700+ pantries, soup kitchens, shelters, and programs throughout Cook County. Part of Feeding America.
address: 4100 W. Ann Lurie Place, Chicago, IL 60632
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Cook County

name: Northern Illinois Food Bank
description: Regional food bank serving 13 counties in Northern Illinois. Distributes food through a network of pantries, soup kitchens, and feeding programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the 13-county Northern Illinois service area

name: Central Illinois Foodbank
description: Regional food bank serving 21 counties in Central Illinois from its Springfield headquarters. Part of the Feeding America network.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the 21-county Central Illinois service area

name: Eastern Illinois Foodbank
description: Regional food bank serving 18 counties in Eastern Illinois. Part of the Feeding America network, operating food pantries and distribution programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the 18-county Eastern Illinois service area

name: River Bend Food Bank
description: Regional food bank serving 23 counties in Western Illinois and Eastern Iowa. Distributes food through a network of pantries and meal programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Western Illinois and Eastern Iowa service area

name: St. Louis Area Foodbank
description: Regional food bank serving 26 counties in Eastern Missouri and Southwestern Illinois including Madison and St. Clair counties. Part of Feeding America.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Southwestern Illinois and Eastern Missouri service area

name: Feeding Illinois
description: Statewide association of Feeding America food banks working together to fight hunger across Illinois. Connects residents to their regional food bank.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Illinois

name: Illinois TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
description: Cash assistance program for low-income families with children. Provides monthly cash benefits while participants work toward self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size
hours: DHS Help Line: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Illinois WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Supplemental nutrition program providing food, nutrition education, and healthcare referrals to pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5 who meet income and nutritional risk guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% FPL

name: Illinois Child Care Assistance Program (CCAP)
description: Helps low-income working families pay for child care so parents can work or attend school. Subsidizes costs at licensed child care providers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families who are working, in school, or in job training and need child care
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 225% FPL for initial eligibility

name: Get Child Care Illinois (CCAP Online Application)
description: Online application portal where Illinois families can apply for child care assistance (CCAP) in under 20 minutes.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families who need help paying for child care
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 225% FPL for initial eligibility

name: RTA Reduced Fare and Ride Free Programs
description: Provides reduced and free transit fares on CTA, Metra, and Pace for seniors 65+, people with disabilities, and low-income riders. Includes the Access Pilot Program for SNAP recipients.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 65+, persons with disabilities, and low-income transit riders
incomeLimitNotes: Ride Free requires qualification through Illinois Benefit Access Program; Access Pilot requires SNAP enrollment

name: CTA Reduced Fare Programs
description: Chicago Transit Authority reduced fare and free ride programs for seniors, students, people with disabilities, and military personnel on CTA buses and trains.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 65+, persons with disabilities, students, and active military
incomeLimitNotes: Free rides for qualifying seniors and disabled through Benefit Access Program

name: Illinois Department of Veterans' Affairs (IDVA)
description: Provides services to Illinois veterans and their families including benefits claims assistance, education benefits, mental health resources, long-term care, and employment support.
address: 69 W. Washington Street, Suite 1000, Chicago, IL 60602
eligibilitySummary: Illinois veterans and their eligible dependents and survivors

name: IDVA Veteran Service Officers
description: Veteran Service Officers across Illinois who help veterans navigate federal and state benefits, file VA claims, and connect to services at no cost.
eligibilitySummary: Illinois veterans and their families
hours: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM; hours vary by office

name: Chicago Department of Family and Support Services (DFSS)
description: Connects Chicago residents to resources for workforce development, senior services, youth programs, homelessness prevention, domestic violence support, and early childhood education.
address: 1615 W. Chicago Avenue, 3rd Floor, Chicago, IL 60622
eligibilitySummary: Chicago residents, with priority for low-income individuals and families
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Chicago 311 City Services
description: Non-emergency city services portal for Chicago residents. Report issues, request services, get information about city programs, and connect to resources online, by phone, or via app.
eligibilitySummary: All Chicago residents and visitors
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Illinois Department on Aging
description: Provides programs and services for older adults and persons with disabilities including home-delivered meals, adult day services, caregiver support, and the Senior HelpLine.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 60 and older and persons with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; some services are available regardless of income

name: Illinois Benefit Access Program
description: Provides free transit rides on CTA, Pace, and Metra and a discount on license plate stickers for seniors 65+ and persons with disabilities who meet income requirements.
eligibilitySummary: Illinois residents age 65+ or persons aged 16+ with disabilities meeting income limits
incomeLimitNotes: Individual income below $33,562; couple income below $44,533

name: Illinois DCFS (Department of Children and Family Services)
description: Protects children from abuse and neglect, provides foster care and adoption services, and supports families in crisis. Report suspected abuse via hotline or online.
eligibilitySummary: Children at risk of abuse or neglect; families needing protective services

name: CARES Crisis Line (Illinois Mental Health Crisis Line)
description: Illinois' Medicaid mental health crisis line providing 24/7 crisis intervention, mobile crisis response coordination, and screening and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Illinois experiencing a mental health crisis; serves both Medicaid and non-Medicaid individuals
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year

name: Illinois Lifeline Telephone Assistance Program
description: Provides discounted or free phone and broadband service to low-income households. Helps eligible residents stay connected with a monthly discount on service.
eligibilitySummary: Households at or below 135% FPL or participating in qualifying assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, etc.)
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 135% FPL or enrolled in qualifying federal assistance programs

name: We Got You Illinois
description: Illinois state resource website helping residents find and apply for public assistance programs including SNAP, WIC, Medicaid, TANF, child care, and more.
eligibilitySummary: All Illinois residents seeking information about available assistance programs

name: Illinois DCEO Workforce Development
description: State agency overseeing workforce development programs, apprenticeships, and American Job Center services across Illinois.
eligibilitySummary: Illinois job seekers, employers, and workforce development partners
